title: Short, medium, long term goals
description: Practice understanding of short, medium, and long terms goals, as well as building a financial plan using these goals.
topic_path: Short, medium, and long term goals

assessment_items.question_content:
Financial goals can be short, medium or long term.

**What is a *short term* financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A goal that you want to achieve in less than a year.
A goal that you want to achieve in more than five years.
A goal that you want to achieve in one to five years.
A goal that you want to achieve at any time.

assessment_items.question_content:
Financial goals can be short, medium or long term.

**What is a *medium term* financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A goal that you want to achieve in less than a year.
A goal that you want to achieve in more than five years.
A goal that you want to achieve in one to five years.
A goal that you want to achieve at any time.

assessment_items.question_content:
Financial goals can be short, medium or long term.

**What is a *long term* financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A goal that you want to achieve in less than a year.
A goal that you want to achieve in more than five years.
A goal that you want to achieve in one to five years.
A goal that you want to achieve at any time.

assessment_items.question_content:
Short, medium, and long term goals are different types of financial goals that you set for yourself based on how much time, money, and risk you need to achieve them.

**What is an example of a *short term* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Short, medium, and long term goals are different types of financial goals that you set for yourself based on how much time, money, and risk you need to achieve them.


**What is an example of a *medium term* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: cutting unnecessary expenses, such as eating out or subscription services
paying off all debt, including student loans and credit cards

assessment_items.question_content:
Short, medium, and long term goals are different types of financial goals that you set for yourself based on how much time, money, and risk you need to achieve them.


**What is an example of a *long term* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: saving for a down payment on a car

assessment_items.question_content:
Jayla wants to buy a new sofa for her apartment by the end of the year. She would like to start setting money aside but is not sure where to keep the money she is saving.

**What would be a good place for Jayla to put her money in, knowing her timeline for this goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Ayesha received her tax refund and wants to put it aside to use for a down payment on a car that she will buy in a year or two.

**What would be a good place for Ayesha to put her money in, knowing her timeline for this goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Mauritius received a bonus check from his employer and wants to put it aside to use for a down payment on a house that he hopes to buy in 7 years. 

**What would be a good place for Mauritius to put his money in, knowing his timeline for this goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A financial plan consists of four main components: a budget, a savings plan, a debt repayment plan, and an investment plan. Read the scenario below, and decide which component it is relevant to.

Maria is a nurse who makes $\$4{,}800$ a month. Each month she does the following:

* She puts $\$500$ into an emergency fund
* She puts $\$200$ in a retirement account
* She puts $\$300$ into her wedding fund. 
* She spends $\$2{,}000$ on rent, food, and bills. 
* She  pays $\$278$ a month towards her student loan debt of $\$25{,}000$



**Where does the $\$300$ she puts in the wedding fund go in her plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A financial plan consists of four main components: a budget, a savings plan, a debt repayment plan, and an investment plan. Read the scenario below, and decide which component it is relevant to.

Frank has been working as a teacher for five years and he makes $\$3{,}500$ a month. Each month he does the following:

* He puts $\$200$ in a retirement account.
* He puts $\$400$ in a savings account for a down payment on a house. 
* He spends $\$1{,}700$ on rent, food, and everyday expenses. 
* He pays $\$300$ a month towards his student loan debt of $\$19{,}000$.

**Where does the $\$1{,}700$ he spends on rent, food, and bills go in his plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A financial plan consists of four main components: a budget, a savings plan, a debt repayment plan, and an investment plan. Read the scenario below, and decide which component it is relevant to.

Romero makes $\$4{,}000$ a month as an engineer. Each month he does the following:

* He puts $\$500$ in a retirement account 
* He puts $\$200$ in a savings account for a vacation 
* He spends $\$2{,}000$ on rent, food, and bills 
* He pays $\$300$ a month towards his student loan debt of $\$22{,}000$

**Where does the $\$500$ he puts in the retirement account  go in his plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
